qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check
description: Update the Quick Start page to reference the `opentabs start` output for MCP client configuration instead of sending users through a manual secret extraction step. The current docs tell users to run `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` and manually replace YOUR_SECRET_HERE, but `opentabs start` already prints ready-to-copy config blocks with the actual secret pre-filled.

userStories.title: Update Quick Start MCP client config section to reference `opentabs start` output
userStories.description: As a new user following the Quick Start guide, I want the MCP client configuration step to tell me to copy the config from the `opentabs start` output, so that I don't waste time running a separate jq command and manually replacing a placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Configure your MCP client' section (step 4) no longer tells users to run `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret`
Instead, it tells users that `opentabs start` (step 2) printed ready-to-copy MCP client config with the secret pre-filled
It mentions `opentabs config show --show-secret` as a way to see the config again later (not as the primary path)
The JSON example block with YOUR_SECRET_HERE is either removed or replaced with a note referring users to the terminal output
The step numbering and flow still make sense after the edit
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx`, lines 35-57. Currently step 4 ('Configure your MCP client') tells users to run `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` to get the secret, then shows a JSON block with YOUR_SECRET_HERE. But step 2 (`opentabs start`) already prints ready-to-copy config blocks for Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and OpenCode — with the actual secret pre-filled.

Rewrite step 4 to:
1. Tell users that `opentabs start` (from step 2) printed ready-to-copy MCP client configuration in the terminal
2. Tell them to copy the appropriate config block (for their client: Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) into their MCP client config file
3. Mention the config file paths for each client (e.g., `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` for Claude Code)
4. Note that `opentabs config show --show-secret` and `opentabs start --show-config` can be used to see the config again at any time
5. Remove the `jq -r .secret` command and the JSON block with YOUR_SECRET_HERE placeholder

The Quick Start docs page also mentions on line 23 that the first-run output includes 'ready-to-copy configuration snippets for popular MCP clients (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenCode) — with your auth secret pre-filled' — so step 4 should reference this rather than contradicting it with a manual extraction path.
